_comment: verify_args.stages[0] reuses the T1 pickplace-triple shape (robot_path/pick_path/place_path/robot_kind) for schema compatibility, but the semantic mapping for this safety canonical is: pick_path = the moving intruder being monitored (HumanProxy), place_path = the robot link being protected (panda_hand wrist). The intruder is not 'picked' and the wrist is not a 'destination' — see Pattern-C in docs/research/2026-05-16-opus-audit-A61-A66.md. Future safety canonicals may want a dedicated verify_args.safety_check block.
goal: Configure a Franka mid-trajectory motion with a clearance monitor armed at a 0.5 m wrist envelope. A moving human-proxy sphere (registered as a moving obstacle for cuRobo replanning) translates into the envelope. When the proxy breaches the threshold the clearance monitor fires and sim_control pauses the timeline, driving joint velocities to zero within 1 simulated second. visualize_clearance renders the safety envelope for debug. Exercises the ISO-15066 speed-and-separation safety-stop pattern — structurally distinct from zone-partition (which uses an exclusion volume + watchdog) by anchoring the threshold on robot link clearance (not zone overlap) and by halting via timeline pause (not teleop watchdog).
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-safety-clearance-monitor-stop:

1) Pattern gap: this is the FIRST canonical to anchor a safety-stop on clearance-to-robot-links rather than on a static exclusion zone (CP-NEW-zone-partition-safety) or corridor collision-avoidance (CP-NEW-multi-amr-corridor). It targets the ISO-15066 speed-and-separation monitoring (SSM) story where the stop threshold scales with proximity to ANY robot link, not a pre-authored volume.

2) Scene geometry:
   - Franka base at [0, 0, 0.75] (on table). Wrist envelope radius 0.5 m per backlog spec.
   - Human-proxy: Sphere at /World/HumanProxy initial position [1.2, 0, 0.95] (well outside 0.5 m envelope from any Franka link).
   - Proxy moves toward robot with linear velocity [-0.30, 0, 0] m/s; at ~2.3 s of sim time it enters the 0.5 m wrist envelope and triggers the stop.

3) set_clearance_monitor:
   - articulation_path = /World/Franka (monitor evaluates min-distance from each link to target_prims).
   - clearance_mm = 500 (0.5 m hard stop — matches backlog spec).
   - warning_mm = 750 (0.75 m warning band for telemetry).
   - target_prims = [/World/HumanProxy] only (Table is static and inside the cell, would false-trigger).

4) Motion: move_to_pose drives the wrist along a forward reach trajectory toward [0.5, 0, 1.10]. This puts the wrist in the path of the incoming proxy so the breach happens during active motion (not at rest) — exercising the 'pause didn't take effect because trajectory was buffered' failure mode.

5) Halt mechanism: set_clearance_monitor only EMITS contact-report classification events; it does NOT auto-bind a halt action. A follow-up run_usd_script step subscribes a second contact-report listener that calls omni.timeline.pause() when the intruder-vs-Franka separation drops below the 0.5 m stop threshold. Pausing the timeline stops physics; joint velocity targets remain but no integration advances, so velocities effectively go to zero from the world frame. This explicit observer/actuator split is the recommended shape for future safety canonicals (watchdog velocity zero, e-stop signal, etc. compose against the same monitor arming step). Distinct from the watchdog approach in CP-NEW-zone-partition-safety which zeroes velocity TARGETS but keeps physics running.

6) register_moving_obstacle: ties the proxy into cuRobo's dynamic-obstacle set so replanning during the breach window avoids the proxy (defensive — even if the human is the one moving, cuRobo treats it as a moving obstacle).

7) visualize_clearance: mode='envelope' renders the 0.5 m sphere around the wrist link. For headless runs, fall back to mode='heatmap' which only writes annotations.

8) Physics: CPU dynamics for determinism (clearance evaluation is per-step; GPU broadphase can race with USD-attribute reads in the monitor callback).

9) get_world_transform on /World/Franka/panda_hand and /World/HumanProxy is used by the diagnose path to verify the actual clearance distance at the moment of breach (instrument-first; do not guess from velocity alone).

10) Verified_status: drafted; function gate requires Kit RPC round-trip to confirm timeline actually pauses on breach (sim_control inside the monitor callback is the risky integration point — see failure_modes).
code:
# ── Ground and lighting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=900.0)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Ground",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, -0.5],
    scale=[20, 20, 1],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic clearance evaluation ────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(
    config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"}
)

# ── Robot mounting table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Table",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, 0.375],
    scale=[0.7, 0.7, 0.375],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Franka arm ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Base at z=0.75 (table top). Default orientation faces +X.
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1.0, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Human-proxy sphere (simulated intruder) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 0.20 m radius sphere starts at [1.2, 0, 0.95] — outside the 0.5 m wrist envelope.
# Will be moved by setting linear_velocity toward the robot wrist to trigger breach.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    prim_type="Sphere",
    position=[1.2, 0, 0.95],
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy", attr_name="radius", value=0.20)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    attr_name="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
# Disable gravity on the proxy so it tracks the commanded linear velocity cleanly.
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    attr_name="physxRigidBody:disableGravity",
    value=True,
)

# ── Register proxy as a moving obstacle for cuRobo replanning ───────────────────────────────
register_moving_obstacle(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    obstacle_path="/World/HumanProxy",
)

# ── Clearance monitor — 0.5 m wrist envelope, 0.75 m warning band ───────────────────────────
# set_clearance_monitor only EMITS contact-report events; the breach-action
# (timeline pause / velocity zero) is the caller's responsibility — see the
# follow-up run_usd_script step that wires omni.physx.subscribe_contact_report_events
# to omni.timeline.pause(). This split keeps the monitor itself a pure observer
# and lets future safety canonicals compose alternate halt mechanisms (watchdog
# velocity zero, e-stop signal, etc.) against the same monitor arming step.
set_clearance_monitor(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    clearance_mm=500.0,
    warning_mm=750.0,
    target_prims=["/World/HumanProxy"],
)

# ── Wire pause-on-breach: subscribe a contact-report listener that fires ─────────────────────
# omni.timeline.pause() the moment the intruder enters the 0.5 m stop zone.
# This is the explicit halt mechanism that realises settle_state.expected_pause_within_s
# and verify_args.expected_pause_on_breach — set_clearance_monitor on its own only
# prints classification messages.
run_usd_script(
    description="Subscribe pause-on-breach callback: when any contact-report separation drops below 0.5 m for a HumanProxy<->Franka link pair, call omni.timeline.pause() and flag breach.",
    code="""
import omni.physx
import omni.timeline

_STOP_THRESHOLD_M = 0.5
_TARGET_PATHS = {"/World/HumanProxy"}
_ROBOT_PATH_PREFIX = "/World/Franka"
_PAUSE_FLAG = {"breached": False, "first_breach_sim_t": None}

_timeline = omni.timeline.get_timeline_interface()
_physx_iface = omni.physx.get_physx_interface()

def _on_contact_report_pause(contact_headers, contact_data):
    if _PAUSE_FLAG["breached"]:
        return
    for header in contact_headers:
        actor0 = str(header.actor0)
        actor1 = str(header.actor1)
        target_hit = (actor0 in _TARGET_PATHS) or (actor1 in _TARGET_PATHS)
        robot_hit = actor0.startswith(_ROBOT_PATH_PREFIX) or actor1.startswith(_ROBOT_PATH_PREFIX)
        if not (target_hit and robot_hit):
            continue
        for i in range(header.contact_data_offset,
                       header.contact_data_offset + header.num_contact_data):
            sep = float(contact_data[i].separation)
            if sep < _STOP_THRESHOLD_M:
                _PAUSE_FLAG["breached"] = True
                _PAUSE_FLAG["first_breach_sim_t"] = _timeline.get_current_time()
                _timeline.pause()
                print(f"[CLEARANCE-PAUSE] breach sep={sep*1000:.1f}mm — timeline paused at sim_t={_PAUSE_FLAG['first_breach_sim_t']:.3f}s")
                return

_clearance_pause_sub = _physx_iface.subscribe_contact_report_events(_on_contact_report_pause)
print("[CLEARANCE-PAUSE] pause-on-breach callback armed (stop_zone=500mm, target=HumanProxy)")
""",
)

# ── Visualize the safety envelope (debug rendering) ─────────────────────────────────────────
visualize_clearance(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    mode="zones",
    target_prims=["/World/HumanProxy"],
    clearance_mm=500.0,
    warning_mm=750.0,
)

# ── Start simulation and command a forward reach trajectory ─────────────────────────────────
sim_control(action="play")

# Drive the wrist forward to position [0.5, 0, 1.10] (extended along +X, table-plane height).
# The trajectory is buffered to physics; the clearance monitor runs every step and will
# fire sim_control("pause") when the proxy crosses the 0.5 m envelope.
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    target_position=[0.5, 0.0, 1.10],
    target_orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
)

# ── Move the human-proxy into the wrist envelope ────────────────────────────────────────────
# At v=[-0.30, 0, 0] m/s, the proxy travels 0.30 m/s × 2.33 s ≈ 0.70 m before crossing the
# 0.5 m envelope around the wrist (initial separation ≈ 1.2 m, wrist forward 0.5 m).
set_linear_velocity(
    prim_path="/World/HumanProxy",
    vel=[-0.30, 0, 0],
)

# ── Diagnose-path hooks — read clearance at the breach moment, do not guess ─────────────────
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Franka/panda_hand")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/HumanProxy")
code_template:
# ── Ground and lighting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=900.0)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Ground",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, -0.5],
    scale=[20, 20, 1],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic clearance evaluation ────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(
    config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"}
)

# ── Robot mounting table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Table",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, 0.375],
    scale=[0.7, 0.7, 0.375],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Robot arm ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="{{primary_robot.class}}",
    dest_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation=[1.0, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Human-proxy sphere ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}",
    prim_type="Sphere",
    position={{human_proxy.initial_position}},
)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", attr_name="radius", value=0.20)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", attr_name="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}", attr_name="physxRigidBody:disableGravity", value=True)

# ── Register proxy as a moving obstacle ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
register_moving_obstacle(
    robot_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    obstacle_path="{{human_proxy.path}}",
)

# ── Clearance monitor — wrist envelope (observer only; pause is wired below) ────────────────
set_clearance_monitor(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    clearance_mm=500.0,
    warning_mm=750.0,
    target_prims=["{{human_proxy.path}}"],
)

# ── Wire pause-on-breach: subscribe contact-report listener that calls timeline.pause() ─────
run_usd_script(
    description="Subscribe pause-on-breach callback for clearance breach.",
    code="""
import omni.physx
import omni.timeline
_STOP_THRESHOLD_M = 0.5
_TARGET_PATHS = {\"{{human_proxy.path}}\"}
_ROBOT_PATH_PREFIX = \"{{primary_robot.path}}\"
_PAUSE_FLAG = {\"breached\": False, \"first_breach_sim_t\": None}
_timeline = omni.timeline.get_timeline_interface()
_physx_iface = omni.physx.get_physx_interface()
def _on_contact_report_pause(contact_headers, contact_data):
    if _PAUSE_FLAG[\"breached\"]: return
    for header in contact_headers:
        a0, a1 = str(header.actor0), str(header.actor1)
        target_hit = (a0 in _TARGET_PATHS) or (a1 in _TARGET_PATHS)
        robot_hit = a0.startswith(_ROBOT_PATH_PREFIX) or a1.startswith(_ROBOT_PATH_PREFIX)
        if not (target_hit and robot_hit): continue
        for i in range(header.contact_data_offset, header.contact_data_offset + header.num_contact_data):
            sep = float(contact_data[i].separation)
            if sep < _STOP_THRESHOLD_M:
                _PAUSE_FLAG[\"breached\"] = True
                _PAUSE_FLAG[\"first_breach_sim_t\"] = _timeline.get_current_time()
                _timeline.pause()
                return
_clearance_pause_sub = _physx_iface.subscribe_contact_report_events(_on_contact_report_pause)
""",
)

# ── Visualize clearance envelope ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
visualize_clearance(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    mode="zones",
    target_prims=["{{human_proxy.path}}"],
    clearance_mm=500.0,
    warning_mm=750.0,
)

# ── Play, command reach, and translate intruder into envelope ───────────────────────────────
sim_control(action="play")
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    target_position=[0.5, 0.0, 1.10],
    target_orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
)
set_linear_velocity(
    prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}",
    vel=[-0.30, 0, 0],
)
get_world_transform(prim_path="{{primary_robot.path}}/panda_hand")
get_world_transform(prim_path="{{human_proxy.path}}")
failure_modes: set_clearance_monitor and the pause-on-breach run_usd_script step must both be invoked before sim_control("play") — if either contact-report subscription is bound after physics starts, the first few steps slip through and the breach moment is missed. Order: arm monitor, wire pause subscriber, then play.

set_clearance_monitor on its own does NOT auto-pause the timeline — it only EMITS contact-report classification messages. The pause-on-breach behavior in settle_state.expected_pause_within_s and verify_args.expected_pause_on_breach is realised by the explicit run_usd_script step that subscribes a second contact-report listener which calls omni.timeline.pause(). Omitting that step leaves the canonical as a pure observer with no halt mechanism.

clearance_mm too tight (e.g. 100 mm) gives false positives from Franka's own joint mesh self-distance evaluation. 500 mm matches ISO-15066 SSM minimum separation and stays clear of self-collision noise.

Pause didn't take effect because trajectory was already buffered to physics: move_to_pose with cuRobo enqueues a full waypoint stream into the articulation drive; sim_control("pause") halts integration but the buffered targets remain — on resume the arm completes the buffered motion. Mitigation: pair the pause with set_joint_targets(current_positions) to flush the buffer.
HumanProxy with gravity enabled falls and the breach happens at the table plane, not at wrist height — disableGravity=True keeps the proxy at z=0.95.
visualize_clearance mode='envelope' requires render context — headless runs should use mode='heatmap' which only writes annotation attributes.

register_moving_obstacle silently no-ops if cuRobo planner state has not been initialised (no prior plan_trajectory/move_to_pose call). Call move_to_pose at least once before register_moving_obstacle to force planner init.

Choosing target_prims=[/World/Table, /World/HumanProxy] in set_clearance_monitor false-triggers immediately because the Franka base is bolted to the table (~0 mm clearance to its own mounting surface). Keep target_prims scoped to the dynamic intruder only.
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; first safety-clearance-monitor canonical (ISO-15066 SSM); pause-on-breach explicitly wired via run_usd_script (subscribe_contact_report_events → omni.timeline.pause); form-gate ✓; function-gate ⏳ (requires Kit RPC to confirm pause callback fires within 1 sim-second of envelope breach)
